content.claim: Hungarian statutory law mandates minimum class size of 26 students (28 recommended, maximum 34) with exceptions only in rare legal cases; as local cohorts fall below these thresholds, forming even single viable gimnázium intake classes becomes numerically untenable, creating mechanical constraint independent of policy preference.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Exemption criteria and frequency of legal waivers in practice not documented in available sources
Interaction with multi-class parallel structures (e.g., whether parallel classes of sub-26 may combine under alternative provision models) unclear
No evidence provided on cost-per-student implications of maintaining undersized cohorts
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
